question: What do Figures 5.4-1 and 5.4-2 reveal about steam generator tube access for inspection?

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale reactor pressurizer, specifying the alloys and standards for each component, such as the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, and bolting. It emphasizes the use of corrosion-resistant materials compliant with ASME BPVC Section II specifications to ensure compatibility and durability in the reactor coolant system.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and emergency valves, and associated piping. It details how these components are integrated within the module, outlines their boundaries between primary and secondary systems, and references other sections for specific design bases and descriptions.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators (SGs) in the NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer, safety valves, and emergency cooling systems. The SG system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the RPV itself forming the outer pressure boundary and the SG tubes contributing to the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards used for threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and stress corrosion cracking resistance. It also outlines the requirements for preservice and inservice inspection and testing of reactor coolant pressure boundary components, specifying adherence to the latest ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) editions as mandated by regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for reactor components.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale Reactor Coolant System's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the main degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that NuScale's novel helical tube and support design does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing validates the new support structure, and typical SG program guidelines remain appropriate, with wear identified as the most likely degradation mechanism and secondary side corrosion risks considered low.

edges.description: Section A references specific components and materials of the pressurizer, while Section B provides a general overview of the reactor pressure vessel and its internal components, including the steam generators, thus Section A specifies details for the broader system described in Section B.

Section A describes the pressurizer materials and references other sections for material details, while Section B provides a general overview of the reactor pressure vessel and its internal components, including the steam generators, thus Section B gives the broader system context for the components detailed in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system components, including the steam generators, while Section B focuses specifically on the steam generators as one of those components, thus Section A's general description is detailed by Section B.

Section A and Section B both discuss materials and design details for components of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, with Section A focusing on threaded fasteners and referencing other sections for further detail, while Section B describes pressurizer materials and also references Section 5.2 for material descriptions, thus both sections are structurally connected through cross-references to shared material specifications.

Section A references Section B by directing the reader to Section 3.13 for further details on the design of threaded fasteners, which are part of the reactor coolant system components described in Section B.

Section A discusses the materials and standards for threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B describes the overall design and components within the reactor pressure vessel, including the steam generators, thus Section A provides specific material details that support the general component descriptions in Section B.

Section B references Section 5.2, which is detailed in Section A, indicating that the material specifications and quality assurance requirements in Section A are directly applicable to the pressurizer components discussed in Section B.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that inform the design and inspection requirements discussed in Section B, which describes the physical configuration and components of the reactor coolant system, including the steam generators relevant to tube access for inspection.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that inform the design and inspection requirements for steam generator tubes, while Section B describes the actual design features of the steam generators, including aspects relevant to tube access for inspection.

Section A discusses observed degradation mechanisms and risk factors for steam generator (SG) tubes, while Section B provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system components, including the presence of steam generators, thus Section B establishes the context for the specific inspection concerns addressed in Section A.

Section A discusses operational experience and degradation mechanisms relevant to steam generator tubes, while Section B provides a general description of the steam generator's integration and components, with both sections offering context necessary to interpret what Figures 5.4-1 and 5.4-2 illustrate about tube access for inspection.

Section A discusses the use and requirements for threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary and cites Section 3.13 for further design details, while Section B lists references that support the technical and regulatory basis for these material and quality assurance requirements.

Section A discusses the material and regulatory compliance of threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B addresses observed degradation mechanisms in steam generator tubing, with both sections connected through their focus on the integrity and inspection requirements of reactor coolant system components.

Section A provides references to industry standards and guidelines that underpin the technical and regulatory basis for the steam generator tube inspection and integrity programs discussed in Section B.
